questions: What is your most treasured memory?
What do you value most in a friendship?
When did you last feel completely at peace — where were you?
What smell instantly takes you back to childhood?
Who made you feel truly seen, and how?
What is something you loved doing as a child that you would love to do again?
Which place feels like home to your body, not just your mind?
What song can always carry you somewhere else?
What is the kindest thing a stranger ever did for you?
What are you quietly proud of that few people know about?
If you could relive one ordinary day, which one would it be?
What does your ideal morning look like?
Who taught you something without knowing they were teaching you?
What is a meal that tastes like love to you?
When did you last laugh until it hurt?
What part of nature calms you the fastest?
What would you tell your ten-year-old self?
What small ritual makes your day feel right?
What is the most beautiful thing you have ever seen with your own eyes?
Whose voice do you love hearing most?
What compliment do you still remember, years later?
What are you learning right now, willingly or not?
What do your hands know how to do that you are grateful for?
When do you feel most like yourself?
Which friendship has shaped who you are today?
What is something you forgave — and felt lighter for it?
What tiny pleasure would you never give up?
What did you once fear that no longer scares you?
Which book or story changed the way you see the world?
What would you make if you knew nobody would judge it?
When did you last try something for the very first time?
What is your favorite sound in the world?
Who would you like to thank — and for what?
Which season feels most like you?
What do you hope people feel after spending time with you?
What is a promise to yourself that you have kept?
Where in your body do you feel joy first?
Which memory do you return to when you need warmth?
What is something beautiful you noticed this week?
If your life had chapters, what would this one be called?
What do you want more of in your everyday life?
What did you inherit — not things, but ways of being?
When did you feel bravest?
Which conversation do you wish could have lasted longer?
What are you curious about lately?
What does rest look like when you do it well?
Who believed in you before you believed in yourself?
Which tradition — old or invented — do you cherish?
What would you do with a completely free afternoon tomorrow?
What is the gentlest advice you have ever received?
Which sky do you remember — a sunset, a storm, the stars somewhere?
What do you love about the age you are now?
If your younger self could see you today, what would amaze them?
What are three things your body did for you today?
